content.claim: Physical capacity in six- and eight-year gimnáziums remained stagnant at approximately 25,962 students in 2024/2025 despite extreme oversubscription (flagship schools receiving 9–26 times more applicants than available places), with one education researcher attributing this to policy decisions redirecting students toward vocational training.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: The causal claim (policy-directed capacity stagnation vs. resource constraint) is attributed to a single interviewed researcher and not independently verified against Ministry budget or staffing documents
Oversubscription statistics are from a limited set of flagship schools; may not represent system-wide pattern
No systematic audit of actual facility capacity constraints across all 6-/8-year gimnaziums
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
